data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent AC3 ("The dual outbound applier paths are collapsed to one (the production path); the retired path is deleted") → 33d0 AC1 ("Exactly one outbound applier path remains in production; the retired path is deleted (no dead, separately-tested applier code)"). FULLY.
- Parent SC2 ("Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path") → 33d0 AC1 + AC2 ("A single test drives fields + comments + labels + links + parent through the production path in one UPDATE and asserts each sub-op took effect"). FULLY.
- Contributes to AC7 (33d0 AC3: no capability regresses; full reconciler suite green) and to SC5/AC6 posture (33d0 AC4: combined test asserts effects/sub-op outcomes, not interaction/mock-call-counts).

Repo cross-check corroborates the child's premise and that its owned criteria are real, scoped work: two parallel outbound appliers exist today — the typed leaf src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py::_apply_outbound_update (routed via typed_dispatch.py / reconcile.py and exercised by tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_outbound_update.py, test_applier_outbound_links.py) versus the batch path in batch_dispatch.py (update_one), whose own comments note links/labels flow "through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf." This is exactly the drift 33d0 collapses; no per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 33d0 itself.

DELEGATION — criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; the declared OUT item (sub-op telemetry → story E / 2359) is consistent with the roster:
- AC1/AC2/SC1 (SSOT snapshot schema; producer→consumer round-trip contract through production path; no silent shape drift) → 8937 (keystone) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures + fake foundation) + 59ef (verified-fake shape fidelity).
- AC4/SC3 (sub-op telemetry; computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly) → 2359.
- AC5/SC4 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip; de-encode 'expected-bug' probe guards) → 822a.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC3 + SC2 FULLY covered by THIS child; AC7 + SC5 contributed; AC1/AC2/SC1 → 8937+fe57+59ef; AC4/SC3 → 2359; AC5/SC4 → 822a; AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence noted below).

ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; ALREADY tracked across siblings 2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57 and on this ticket via prior G3 audits — NOT re-filed as a new finding here to avoid duplication): Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including 33d0 (AC4), only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests. Repo search confirms docs/mutation-testing.md is a general mutation-testing doc, not the AC6 reconciler-test governing artifact, and no child references producing/extending it. Suggested home: keystone 8937, or a small dedicated child.

No NEW coverage finding for this pairing: child 33d0's owned criteria (AC3/SC2) are FULLY covered, and the single roster-level AC6 gap is already tracked across the sibling set.
